notes: Anonymous block of 6 uniform 5-vertex LWPOLYLINEs on xref-bound INSUL layer - insulation batt fill fragment of the base plan; no wall boundary lines present.

rationale.evidence: All 6 entities are LWPOLYLINEs with exactly 5 vertices on the single xref-bound layer X-평면도(기본형)$0$INSUL (sampled handles 483F through 4844). The uniform 5-vertex signature repeated across the block is the classic zigzag batt-insulation motif laid along a wall cavity run. There are no LINE, TEXT, DIMENSION, or HATCH entities, so the block carries plan fill content only.
rationale.rule: An anonymous *U block whose contents are exclusively short, equal-vertex-count polylines on an insulation (INSUL) layer bound from a plan xref is an insulation-fill fragment of the plan drawing, not a standalone symbol, furniture item, or dimension cache.
rationale.counterfactual: If these polylines sat on a wall or centerline layer, or if long paired parallel LINEs appeared alongside them, I would raise wall_likelihood sharply and nominate those as wall lines; if TEXT or DIMENSION entities were present the block would shift toward 치수캐시.
rationale.uncertainty: Vertex coordinates are not exposed, so the zigzag interpretation rests on the layer name and the 5-vertex-per-polyline signature alone; the actual extent, orientation, and which wall cavity the insulation run occupies cannot be determined from this projection.

notes: Composite plan symbol: 1120x126 line band (two 50-apart parallel chords with symmetric end caps) + 2 WIPEOUTs + 국기계양대 insert - reads as a window/railing band with flag-pole holder, not raw wall geometry.

rationale.evidence: Twenty LINEs on layer 0 form a symmetric 1120x126 band: two long parallel chords at y=-50.5 and y=-100.5 spanning x=±538 (50 apart), an outer frame reaching x=±560 with jamb-like end caps, plus 2 WIPEOUTs masking the background and one INSERT of block X-평면도(기본형)$0$국기계양대 (flag-pole holder). The wipeout+insert+thin-band composition reads as a manufactured plan-view fixture (window/railing band with attached flag-holder), not loose plan linework.
rationale.rule: An anonymous block that combines background WIPEOUTs, a named fixture INSERT, and a mirror-symmetric thin band of parallel lines with end jambs is a composite architectural symbol (opening/railing fixture) rather than wall geometry, even when its long chords superficially resemble wall lines.
rationale.counterfactual: If the two long parallel chords were spaced at a typical wall thickness (100-200 units) on a wall layer, or extended beyond the block extents without end jambs, I would reclassify them as true wall lines and the block as 평면 부분도; removing the 국기계양대 insert would leave a plain window/railing band.
rationale.uncertainty: This projection cannot show whether the 50-unit gap is a railing, window frame, or a thin wall leaf, nor the insertion context (exterior wall, parapet, or balcony edge), so the wall-vs-fixture reading of the long chords stays moderately uncertain.
